name: Fish Stock Recipe
steps: Make 10% salt water brine. Take the gill off from all the fish. Clean fish and place in salty brine water for 30 minutes.
Prepare a big pot of boiling water in the meantime.
Take out the carcasses from brine water and blanch them for 10 seconds, doing 3 carcasses at a time.
Once done, take out and place in cold water, then wash blood out from spine and all scum from inside.
Do not leave them in running water, just wash quickly and immediately drain.
While doing the above process, prepare another stock pot with 10 liters of filtered water and bring to a boil.
Once water is boiling, place smashed ginger and spring onion, and 10 fish carcasses in the pot. Bring to a boil and simmer for about 1 hour.
The temperature of simmering should be enough just for bubbles coming up to the surface.
Keep skimming frequently and remove all dirt while cooking, as water circulates well, it extracts better taste.
Make sure you skim well and maintain correct temperature of stock for better and cleaner taste.
When your stock is ready and tastes right, take all ingredients out and pass through muslin cloth into another pot.
It seems very thin compared to western style stock, but that’s what you are looking for: a clean, light flavor.
Concentrate more on the cleanness and freshness of the taste through your gorge and nose.
Remember, stock making is the basic of a chef in Asia, and it only needs your attention and care for food.
